text:
Unknown: Hey, Gina, can you hear me?
Unknown: Yeah, I can hear you.
Unknown: How are you?
Unknown: I'm good, I'm good.
Unknown: Can you hear me now?
Unknown: Yeah, yeah, I can, sir.
Unknown: I'm just putting on my headphones.
Unknown: One second.
Unknown: I guess it's okay.
Unknown: How.
Unknown: How's the day?
Unknown: You're busy, right?
Unknown: January busy, man.
Unknown: Oh yeah.
Unknown: Oh yeah, I have.
Unknown: Hey, am I coming through perfect?
Unknown: Yeah, yeah.
Unknown: I have more.
Unknown: I feel like I have more opportunities than hour hours in a day, which is a very lucky thing to be able to say, but comes with the downside of, you know, lots of.
Unknown: Yeah.
Unknown: So thanks for joining the call.
Unknown: How do I pronounce her name again?
Unknown: Virgin.
Unknown: Virgin.
Unknown: Bridge.
Unknown: Like bridge?
Unknown: Bridge, yeah.
Unknown: Bridge.
Unknown: Virgin, V. Whatever's easiest.
Unknown: I think Bridge is easiest for me.
Unknown: Yeah, it's okay.
Unknown: Dealer's choice.
Unknown: I'm.
Unknown: I think that's one of the upsides of having a little bit of a hard to pronounce name.
Unknown: You become pretty flexible about how it's pronounced.
Unknown: Yeah.
Unknown: And I think like Americans also find it hard if it's hard to pronounce it kind of sometimes struggle.
Unknown: Yeah, yeah.
Unknown: I mean, Gina is okay, but I feel like your middle name probably gives Americans.
Unknown: Yeah, I get a lot of that.
Unknown: So.
Unknown: Yeah, you could just see their eyes go like.
Unknown: And you just know.
Unknown: Yeah.
Unknown: Even my ex company is FUA Collective and they will always be F U E and they don't dare to say it out loud, so it's so funny.
Unknown: Oh my God.
Unknown: Yeah, it's.
Unknown: It's.
Unknown: It's one of those things.
Unknown: Right?
Unknown: Our brain has autocomplete as well.
Unknown: It's not just the.
Unknown: Yeah, yeah.
Unknown: So thanks for joining this call.
Unknown: So first I want to know more about like what you're doing now and your stage and how this community can help you.
Unknown: Let me share some things about the community first, please.
Unknown: Yeah, I think you heard a little bit about it, but last time when we met, I think I didn't introduce it because I wasn't really like 100% into it now.
Unknown: I was 100% into it.
Unknown: It started one year ago.
Unknown: There's 300 people inside now, mainly founders, operators, advisors.
Unknown: And people are all over the world.
Unknown: Some from Canada, a few from Singapore, Malaysia and even Europe and us as well.
Unknown: So it's a bit of a mixed community.
Unknown: So people that are looking to either give up their product or enter the US market.
Unknown: There are also a lot of stateside companies, but a lot of them are founders, mainly solo founders, bootstrap founders, or founders without the VC funding or with funding.
Unknown: But they're working like themselves without a team.
Unknown: These are the profile founders.
Unknown: So it's created because if the mindset that as a founder it's very hard to make decisions that are in very stressful environment, especially when we have to answer to stakeholders or investors, usually a few.
Unknown: And below it's just a team, your interns, your employees.
Unknown: So there isn't really a middle ground where you can have this peer group where you can go and discuss a burning topic that you want to do for your startup before you actually take it.
Unknown: For example, if you want to price a product for the US market for New York and you don't know whether which one is the best pricing, so you bring together similar stage founders who have done it before for the discussion.
Unknown: So you can actually know how other people price it and you can then decide like, oh, this is actually what I want to do based on personal insights.
Unknown: Because this kind of information you can't get from AI, you can't get online as well.
Unknown: Right, right.
Unknown: And all the decision ports that we have for discussions, we will also do a recording of the meeting with a summary, anonymize the answers like for example the founder's name or any numbers or companies.
Unknown: But the details of the decision and the decision made will be sent to people and members in the community.
Unknown: Community as well.
Unknown: Very.
Unknown: So this is, yeah, this is the first part of the offering.
Unknown: Second one we have advisor call, so we have some people from Google and Nero that join us as advisors.
Unknown: So they'll provide free 15 minute sessions to our founders, like one call per month.
Unknown: And you can go more if you want for any paid engagement.
Unknown: But they will provide all these free calls.
Unknown: Like if you need to answer a burning question now, then you can just hop onto a call with them.
Unknown: You don't need to like pay for 15 minutes and to find someone for two hours just for this question to be answered.
Unknown: So this is second value that we give to the members.
Unknown: And third one is just we are building up members discount and all these special perks that we have that tools you need to go to market or growth.
Unknown: But that's like still in progress.
Unknown: So it just started this year and there's quite a lot of people inside for this port and it's not really a program.
Unknown: People think it's kind of a thing that you have to join limited, but it's kind of a monthly pot session where you get all the perks, advices, calls and once a month or twice a month you go into groups with founders that are solving the same problem as you to get the answer to the problem.
Unknown: And after two, three months you'll come back and you will share the findings and answers so you can actually know like what works, what don't work.
Unknown: So you don't go to the wrong route of wasting your budget.
Unknown: You know, spend so much cash on something that you don't know whether it works or not.
Unknown: Right.
Unknown: Yeah.
Unknown: What I find really interesting is that you're solving a problem with communities which is that you put a lot of people in one room but you don't necessarily generate value.
Unknown: Right.
Unknown: And value can also be locked away in sort of small subsets of the people that do talk to each other and ex, you know, the sort of, the rest are excluded.
Unknown: So I like that, I like the idea of something that disseminates learnings and sort of keeps keeps up with you and lets you like articulate what you've learned over the long run.
Unknown: Yeah.
Unknown: And so we also do like, we are looking to do like partnerships and shared distribution.
Unknown: I don't know if you're doing that for your business, but as you later, because a lot of startups, they have limited budget and if you want to do B2B partnerships it takes like six months.
Unknown: They go discussion sourcing and ours is already a warm community, that passing stage.
Unknown: So you can basically like very easily find another founder who is willing to close a B2B partnership distribution channel.
Unknown: You can go half the budget for marketing spread out 2 times channel then actually, you know, it saves a lot of money I think in the long run.
Unknown: So now what is the, is there any channel for, or avenue for helping other members of the community with their hiring needs?
Unknown: Now we don't have, but probably we can do some partnerships with people.
Unknown: There are quite a few founders who want to raise funding so I have already raised.
Unknown: But most of them, you know, they enter market so they will probably need some talents in the next three to six months.
Unknown: So what Careerspan does, just to remind you, give you some context, we use conversational coaching to help candidates figure out their value prop, sort of who they are, what their best attributes are.
Unknown: And then we take that data and we match them to jobs with that conversational, with those conversational insights.
Unknown: And so we're also linking up with a recruiting agency in India to give folks access to really high quality us proven engineering talent in like short notice, you know.
Unknown: So I'm hoping that that will be something that we can, I'm hoping that that's something that we can deliver on for like your members or just for anyone else.
Unknown: What are the parameters around joining the community?
Unknown: Like required participation, dues, any of that stuff?
Unknown: You mean if you join a community?
Unknown: Yeah, yeah.
Unknown: Like are there any other details to joining the community?
Unknown: No, not really.
Unknown: So now maybe just I will just onboard people based on their goals.
Unknown: Like current go to market goals for example.
Unknown: Like what you're doing now.
Unknown: Another founder maybe is doing a similar thing that is complementary but not your competitor and it can be something you can work with them to do it instead of just doing it yourself and spending 100 of the budget.
Unknown: Yeah, there's basically no strong nose that the most one prerequisite is focused on the US market.
Unknown: If you're really considering US market.
Unknown: Any city.
Unknown: So that's one and software company.
Unknown: Yeah, yeah, I think that, I mean that's totally doable for us.
Unknown: We're in the US market, we're software company.
Unknown: I think that works.
Unknown: If you have folks that are recruiters or recruitment based orgs in your community that would be the main objective.
Unknown: Like connecting with recruiters would be awesome.
Unknown: Secondarily connecting with founders that want to hire overseas engineers would be awesome.
Unknown: Those are sort of the most meaningful things for our go to market right now.
Unknown: So are you focused on B2B or B2C as well?
Unknown: So we have a B2C product, we have the career coaching product, but we're monetizing B2B.
Unknown: So you have already launched for B2B?
Unknown: Yeah, yeah.
Unknown: So we're working with recruiters, working with companies, know gang folks placed as we speak.
Unknown: So do you have any industry focus?
Unknown: Just no industry focus, just sort of engineers with US work experience or work experience at an American company.
Unknown: So they are engineers from outside of the US Coming to the US or.
Unknown: They'Re Indian engineers in India but that have worked with or in U.S. environments.
Unknown: And so they are ready to sort of deploy in a way that is far greater or is better than the average Indian engineer even.
Unknown: So are you actually helping them to move into the US or just be remote?
Unknown: Just be remote.
Unknown: I mean we could do either depending on whether the company wants to bring them in.
Unknown: But our my real value add is sort of the career span side is to produce the matching.
Unknown: Right.
Unknown: Is to save the employers from looking at 99 terrible resumes.
Unknown: To read one good resume it is to allow them to read four resumes and they're all great and they're all candidates that they want to interview.
Unknown: Okay, that's interesting.
Unknown: And so you have launched already for two years, right?
Unknown: Yeah, so we have a couple Thousand users on right now and are building out a talent pipeline of engineering talent.
Unknown: So we're hoping to hit about 100 engineers by the end of the month.
Unknown: Okay, so do you mainly focus just on engineers for now?
Unknown: Yeah.
Unknown: Just niching in for a little bit.
Unknown: Okay.
Unknown: This is interesting.
Unknown: I have a company that is doing similar to you, but she focused on immigration.
Unknown: Let me try to find a profile.
Unknown: I mean, that'd be kind of a cool match.
Unknown: Yeah, I sent you the link here.
Unknown: Have you heard of Emeria Victoria?
Unknown: Victoria.
Unknown: What they do is also very unique.
Unknown: They help with hiring, but they focus on navigating the immigration system.
Unknown: So they also targeting B2B and they have a B2C model already, but they help with immigration.
Unknown: Yeah, they have an immigration process.
Unknown: So I think it'll be good.
Unknown: I can connect you with her.
Unknown: She's in the community.
Unknown: Yeah.
Unknown: No, would love to be connected with her.
Unknown: I think there's a.
Unknown: There's a lot of synergies there.
Unknown: Yeah.
Unknown: Yeah, if you could do that, that would be awesome.
Unknown: Yeah.
Unknown: Because she's also looking for like you.
Unknown: They started with B2B and they're expanding the.
Unknown: They started with B2C.
Unknown: Sorry.
Unknown: They're expanding the B2B.
Unknown: The companies and it's more complicated.
Unknown: Right.
Unknown: Yeah.
Unknown: So they are also like doing this.
Unknown: I think it's quite interesting because it's kind of connecting.
Unknown: But yours is not immigration.
Unknown: Yes.
Unknown: It's immigrate policies, these things.
Unknown: But seems like we could just pass folks to each other, right?
Unknown: Yeah.
Unknown: Like partner partnership.
Unknown: Exactly, exactly.
Unknown: I think that would be encouraging.
Unknown: So I will.
Unknown: Yeah, that would be.
Unknown: That would be awesome.
Unknown: I actually am getting pinged by my.
Unknown: I really apologize.
Unknown: Getting pinged by my head of AI So I have to jump.
Unknown: But you know, if.
Unknown: Would love to join the community.
Unknown: You know, I think it's.
Unknown: It's really cool sort of space and would like to participate in some of this programming as well.
Unknown: And then we can take it from there.
Unknown: I will send you an email with the details and also I'll reach out to Victoria to connect with her.
Unknown: Yeah.
Unknown: Amazing.
Unknown: Well, thank you so much, Gina.
Unknown: It's a real pleasure.
Unknown: Thanks for the call.
Unknown: Have a great day and great week.
Unknown: Thank you.
Unknown: Catch you soon.
Unknown: Bye.
Unknown: Bye.
title: xfy-mvrc-rti

summary.action_items:
**Virgin Kim**
Onboard Gina into the community based on her US market and software company focus (08:29)
Send Gina an email with community details and onboarding information (12:40)
Connect Gina with Emeria Victoria from the community who focuses on immigration-related hiring services for possible partnership (11:21)

**Gina Vrijen**
Join the community and participate in upcoming programming to explore collaborative opportunities (12:43)
summary.shorthand_bullet:
👋 **Introduction** (00:11 - 00:30)
Casual exchange on names and pronunciation between Gina and Virgin.
Setting a friendly tone for the meeting.
🌍 **Community Overview** (02:00 - 02:45)
Community established about a year ago with 300 members.
Members include solo, bootstrap founders, operators, and advisors from various countries.
💬 **Monthly Pot Sessions** (03:16 - 05:00)
Founders discuss challenges like product pricing in the US market.
Facilitates knowledge sharing and avoids costly mistakes.
📞 **Advisory Calls** (04:32 - 05:30)
Advisors from companies like Google provide free 15-minute calls monthly.
Enhances startup support for community members.
🤝 **B2B Partnerships** (06:30 - 07:15)
Community fosters partnerships for B2B distribution.
Members can share marketing channels and budgets.
💼 **Hiring Support Discussion** (07:00 - 08:00)
Potential to support hiring needs within the community.
Introduction of Careerspan for building strong candidate value propositions.
🔍 **Joining Criteria** (08:20 - 09:00)
Focus on US market orientation and software companies.
Partnership opportunities with complementary goals.
💰 **Careerspan's Monetization** (09:40 - 10:40)
B2B monetization working with recruiters and companies.
Focus on remote work rather than immigration services.
🌐 **Synergies with Emeria Victoria** (11:20 - 12:00)
Exploration of synergies between Careerspan and Emeria Victoria.
Potential partnership for complementary services.
✅ **Next Steps** (12:40 - 13:06)
Agreement for Gina to be onboarded into the community.
Virgin to connect Gina with Emeria Victoria.
summary.overview:
- **Community Purpose:** Peer support network for solo founders in the US focuses on real-world insights, not generic advice.  
- **Membership Growth:** 300 members globally, facilitating knowledge sharing and informal ongoing sessions tailored by problem type.  
- **Expert Support:** Monthly 15-minute advisor calls from professionals help founders with urgent questions without costly commitments.  
- **Recruitment Needs:** Members plan to hire within three to six months; potential partnerships with recruiting services recognized as essential.  
- **Talent Matching:** Careerspan targets Indian engineers for US roles, aiming to place 100 engineers this month while improving hiring quality.  
- **Community Values:** No formal dues; prioritizes founders with US market goals, fostering collaboration without competitive conflicts.
